content.full_text:
CITY OF CONCORD
1950 Parkside Drive, MS/08
Concord, California 94519-2578
FAX: (510) 798-0636

Michael D. Martello
City Attorney

Laurel Reiterman Weil
Assistant City Attorney

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
Telephone: (510) 671-3160

Mark T. Boehme
Deputy City Attorney

Concord

March 30, 1992

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Opinion with Respect to Conflict of Interest Issue (Fred Doster)

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This office hereby requests the Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC) for a formal
opinion with respect to whether or not Fred Doster, a member of the City of Concord's
Redevelopment Agency Advisory Committee, has a conflict of interest that would preclude
him from participating in deliberations on a project pending before the City of Concord's
Redevelopment Agency.

FACTUAL BACKGROUND

The Redevelopment Agency Advisory Committee ("RAC") was created in February 1976.
Guidelines were adopted per Redevelopment Agency Resolution No. 78-44, a copy of which
is attached. It sets forth the purpose, function, and operation of the Committee. A copy
of the guidelines is also attached. There are eleven members authorized to serve on the
"RAC" and are appointed by the Redevelopment Agency ("Agency"). The guidelines set
forth a preference of members for the West Concord area, downtown area, and at large
members. The Agency created the "RAC" for the purpose of reviewing the formulation and
implementation of redevelopment plans and projects. The "RAC" is not a project area
advisory committee within the meaning of Health & Safety Code §33385.

The guidelines further define and clarify the purpose, function, and scope of responsibility
of the "RAC." The following items are the major responsibilities of the "RAC":

1) To review and make recommendations to the Redevelopment Agency on
general and specific redevelopment plans and implementation programs
initiated by the Agency or Agency staff;

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
Re: Request for Opinion with Respect to Conflict of Interest Issue
March 30, 1992

Page 2

2) To review, evaluate, and make recommendations to the Agency on privately initiated new construction developments and major expansion or remodeling of existing structures within the Redevelopment project area as to the potential for Agency participation;

3) To make recommendations to the Redevelopment Agency on budget preparation and priorities.

The "RAC" and the Agency have been reviewing a major project proposed for downtown Concord, which is located within the Redevelopment project area. The proposed project, if developed, would be a "first class" mixed use development containing a ten (10) screen theater complex, retail shops, offices, and residential units.

Mr. Doster, a member of the "RAC", holds a management position and is an officer of a retail shopping center which is adjacent to the proposed project. He also conducts leasing activity at the shopping center and is compensated on a commission basis. The member's shopping center recently converted its older one (1) screen theater to a three (3) screen configuration. Although physically a part of the member's center, the member exercises no management control over the theater parcel or operation.

A question arose as to whether this person could participate as a "RAC" member dealing with the proposed project in light of his management position and leasing activity at the shopping center, as well as the shopping center being a source of income to the member.

Our office's analysis of the issue concluded that: 1) The member is a "public official" within the meaning of the PRA (§82048); 2) The "RAC" is a "Local Governmental Agency" within the meaning of the PRA (§83041); 3) He holds a management position; and 4) It was reasonably foreseeable, under the PRA, that a decision on the project could have a material financial effect on the member's economic interest, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally within the meaning of Government Code §87103(c) and (d).

The "RAC" member has denied any potential for a conflict of interest with respect to his participation in this matter. Copies of the initial advice letter dated January 2, 1992 from our office and the follow-up letter dated March 13, 1992 are attached hereto. Correspondence from the "RAC" member dated February 24, 1992 and March 18, 1992 is also attached hereto setting forth his reasoning why there is no conflict of interest.

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
Re:    Request for Opinion with Respect to Conflict of Interest Issue
March 30, 1992

Page 3

Although the "RAC" is an advisory committee, it is, and has been throughout this particular project, an integral part of the decision-making process with respect to the advice it gives to the Agency Board and it is anticipated that it will continue to have significant input with respect to this project as well as future projects within the Redevelopment area.

It is my understanding that turnaround time on requests for opinions is approximately three weeks from the time that they are received by the FPPC. We will appreciate any effort to expedite this request opinion insofar as the time constraints facing the Agency with respect to a decision on the project including the deliberation and recommendation of the "RAC" must be completed within four (4) weeks.

Please feel free to contact me at (510) 671-3161 if you have any questions, comments, or need further information with respect to this request.

Sincerely yours,

MICHAEL D. MARTELLO
City Attorney

By:    MARK T. BOEHME
       Deputy City Attorney

MTB:mam

Attachments

cc:    City Manager
       Director of Economic & Community Development

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Fred Doster, a member of the City of Concord's Redevelopment Agency Advisory Committee, has a conflict of interest that would preclude him from participating in deliberations on a major mixed-use development project, given his management position and leasing activity at an adjacent shopping center that could be financially affected by the project.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a local government entity to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No verbatim QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The document contains detailed factual background and legal analysis by the requesting party. Document includes attachments (prior advice letters and member correspondence) referenced but not included in the provided text.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Concord requests a formal FPPC opinion regarding whether a Redevelopment Agency Advisory Committee member has a conflict of interest in participating in decisions regarding a downtown development project, given his financial interest in an adjacent shopping center. The City Attorney's office has already concluded that a material conflict likely exists under Government Code Section 87103(c) and (d), but the committee member disputes this finding.
